marketFocus.focus: RBA Hikes 25bps, Precious Metals Rebound, ISM Manufacturing Expansion
headlines: RBA hikes Cash Rate by 25bps to 3.85% (unanimous decision)—inflation pulse too strong, hurts all Australians
Precious metals stage comeback: Gold +2.1%, Silver +3.5% in early Asia-Pac trade after historic sell-off
ISM Manufacturing PMI 52.6 vs exp. 48.5 (first expansion in 12 months)—New Orders 57.1 vs prev. 47.4
Asia-Pac stocks rebound: Nikkei 225 +3%, KOSPI biggest gainer, risk-on sentiment returns
marketFocus.macroContext: Risk appetite returns as markets digest better-than-expected ISM Manufacturing data and RBA hawkish hike. US stocks saw gains to start the week (Consumer Staples, Industrials led), while Energy/Utilities/Real Estate lagged. Warsh Fed Chair nomination continues to support USD strength. Partial government shutdown persists—BLS delays January NFP report to Friday, December JOLTS report to Tuesday. Materials names see notable strength as Trump launches USD 12bln mineral stockpile to counter China.

tradingSummary: Dollar takes a breather after strengthening post-ISM data—all focus turns to RBA decision. DXY rangebound, AUD hit on dented metals initially but recovers post-RBA hike. Precious metals rebound sharply (+2-3.5%) following crowded bull rally profit-taking. Asia-Pac stocks extend advances with Nikkei 225 +3%+, KOSPI in broad turnaround from yesterday's bloodbath. T-note futures languish at prior day's trough after recent flattening post-ISM data. WTI dips below USD 62/bbl as US-Iran tensions ease (talks in Turkey this week looming). Strong Risk-On detected (12 Risk ON Factors).

USD.summary: Dollar started the week firm against all G10 peers as sentiment on the back continued to improve following Kevin Warsh Fed Chair nomination. Market participants digested better-than-expected ISM Manufacturing PMI data (52.6 vs exp. 48.5)—first expansion in 12 months, supported by rise in New Orders (57.1 vs prev. 47.4) and Production (55.9 vs prev. 50.7). Fed's Bostic argued one or two rate cuts would put them at neutral, said premature to say inflation job is done. Partial government shutdown persists—BLS delayed January NFP report to Friday, December JOLTS report to Tuesday. DXY takes breather but Warsh hawkish premium intact. 1-3d: Bullish bias, focus on JOLTs (16:00) and Warsh speech Wed 16:15.
headlines: ISM Manufacturing PMI 52.6 vs exp. 48.5—first expansion in 12 months
Warsh Fed Chair nomination continues to support USD strength
BLS delays January NFP to Friday due to government shutdown
JOLTs Job Openings forecast 7.1M (prev. 7.146M) at 16:00

EUR.summary: Euro little changed despite USD strength—EUR/USD rangebound around 1.04. German Retail Sales MoM 0.1% vs prev. -0.5%, YoY 1.5% vs prev. 1.3% show modest improvement. Final Manufacturing PMIs confirm contraction: Germany 49.1 (prev. 47.0), France 49.5 (prev. 48.8), Eurozone 49.5 (prev. 48.8). ECB's Lagarde to speak at informal dinner with EU Commissioner Sefcovic Wed 19:00 (no text). French Budget Balance and Inflation Rate YoY Prel due Tue 08:45. Eurozone Core Inflation Rate YoY Flash Wed 11:00 (forecast 2.3%). 1-3d: Neutral bias, data-dependent, watch inflation prints Wed.
headlines: German Retail Sales improve: 0.1% M/M vs prev. -0.5%
Eurozone Manufacturing PMI Final 49.5—contraction persists
Core Inflation Rate YoY Flash Wed 11:00 (forecast 2.3%)
Lagarde to speak Wed 19:00 at informal dinner

GBP.summary: Sterling little changed vs USD—GBP/USD around 1.25. Nationwide Housing Prices YoY 1.0% vs prev. 0.6%, MoM 0.3% vs prev. -0.4% show housing market stabilization. S&P Global Manufacturing PMI Final 51.8 vs prev. 50.6—expansion territory. BoE's Breeden delivered keynote speech at regulation summit Mon 12:45, exploring retail payments without cards. GBP 4.25bln 4.75% 2035 Gilt Auction Tue 11:00. S&P Global Services PMI Final Wed 10:30 key test. 1-3d: Neutral bias, manufacturing recovery positive but USD strength caps gains.
headlines: S&P Global Manufacturing PMI Final 51.8—expansion territory
Nationwide Housing Prices YoY 1.0% vs prev. 0.6%
BoE's Breeden explores retail payments without cards
Services PMI Final Wed 10:30 key test

JPY.summary: Yen weakens as risk-on sentiment returns—USD/JPY around 150.50. Japan sells JPY 1.99tln 10yr JGBs, b/c 3.20x (prev. 3.30x), average yield 2.249% (prev. 2.095%)—yields rising. S&P Global Manufacturing PMI Final 51.5 vs prev. 50.0 shows modest expansion. T-note futures languish at prior day's trough after recent flattening post-ISM data. Safe-haven bid fades as Asia-Pac stocks rebound sharply. JPY 2.6tln 10-year JGB Auction Tue 04:35. 1-3d: Neutral bias, defensive bid fades on risk-on, watch US yields.
headlines: Japan 10yr JGB auction: yield 2.249% vs prev. 2.095%
USD/JPY around 150.50 as risk-on returns
S&P Global Manufacturing PMI Final 51.5—modest expansion
Safe-haven bid fades on Asia-Pac stocks rebound

AUD.summary: Aussie initially hit on dented metals but recovers post-RBA hawkish hike. RBA hikes Cash Rate by 25bps to 3.85% (unanimous decision, as expected)—states inflation likely to remain above target for some time, pulse of inflation too strong, high inflation hurts all Australians. Governor Bullock says does not know if this will be a tightening cycle, cannot rule anything out or in. Building Permits MoM Prel -14.9% vs exp. -5.7% (prev. 15.2%)—construction sector weak. ANZ-Indeed Job Ads MoM 4.4% vs prev. -0.8% shows labor market resilience. 1-3d: Bullish bias, RBA hawkish stance supports AUD, watch Building Approvals Wed.
headlines: RBA hikes Cash Rate 25bps to 3.85%—inflation pulse too strong
Governor Bullock: cannot rule anything out or in
Building Permits MoM Prel -14.9% vs exp. -5.7%
ANZ-Indeed Job Ads MoM 4.4%—labor market resilient

NZD.summary: Kiwi rangebound—NZD/USD around 0.58. Building Permits MoM -4.6% vs prev. 2.7% shows construction sector weakness. Labour Costs Index QoQ forecast 0.5% (prev. 0.5%), Employment Change QoQ forecast 0.3% (prev. 0.0%), Unemployment Rate forecast 5.3% (prev. 5.3%) due Tue 22:45. S&P Global Services PMI Final Wed 23:00. 1-3d: Neutral bias, labor market data Tue 22:45 key test.
headlines: Building Permits MoM -4.6% vs prev. 2.7%
Labour Costs Index QoQ forecast 0.5% due Tue 22:45
Employment Change QoQ forecast 0.3%
Unemployment Rate forecast 5.3%

CAD.summary: Loonie rangebound—USD/CAD around 1.43. WTI dips below USD 62/bbl as US-Iran tensions ease (talks in Turkey this week looming). Crude benchmarks hit: WTI (H6) settles USD 3.07 lower at 62.14/bbl, Brent (J6) settles USD 3.02 lower at 66.30/bbl. S&P Global Manufacturing PMI 50.4 vs prev. 48.6 shows modest expansion. Weekly Private Crude Stocks (30 Jan, w/e) due Tue 22:30. 1-3d: Neutral bias, oil price sensitive, watch crude inventory data Tue.
headlines: WTI dips below USD 62/bbl on US-Iran tensions easing
Crude benchmarks settle 3+ lower—WTI 62.14/bbl
S&P Global Manufacturing PMI 50.4 vs prev. 48.6
Weekly Private Crude Stocks due Tue 22:30

CHF.summary: Franc weakens as risk-on sentiment returns—USD/CHF around 0.91. Swiss Retail Sales MoM 1.0% vs prev. -0.1%, YoY 2.9% vs prev. 1.7% show strong consumer demand. procure.ch Manufacturing PMI 48.8 vs prev. 46.4—contraction eases. SNB's Chairman Schlegel says interest rates currently appropriate, SNB can cut rates below zero but higher threshold now, sees inflation at 0.7% in 2026 and 2027. Safe-haven bid fades on risk-on. 1-3d: Neutral bias, risk-on weighs on CHF.
headlines: Swiss Retail Sales YoY 2.9% vs prev. 1.7%—strong consumer demand
SNB's Schlegel: rates currently appropriate
procure.ch Manufacturing PMI 48.8—contraction eases
Safe-haven bid fades on risk-on sentiment
